rulings.Case Title: JESSIE MENDOZA et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION Notice Of Defendants American President Lines, Ltd., Crowley Maritime Corporation, Matson Navigation Company, Inc., Maersk, Inc. And Nedlloyd Lines (Usa) Corp.¿S Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs¿ Third Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, March 13, 2009, line 1, Defendants American President Lines, Ltd., Crowley Maritime Corporation, Matson Navigation Company, Inc., Maersk, Inc. And Nedlloyd Lines (Usa) Corp.'s Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs' Third Amended Complaint. Defendants' motion to strike is denied. Defendants previously filed a motion to strike portions of plaintiffs' Third Amended Complaint, which was granted without leave to amend on Decmeber 22, 2008. Judge Mahoney's January 28, 2009 Order overruling Defendants' Demurrers did not allow Defendants to file another motion to strike. Judge Mahoney's Order stated only that Defendants should have addressed the extraneous allegations in the motion to strike that was filed, not in the Demurrers. Judge Mahoney has confirmed that he did not intend to allow Defendants to bring another motion to strike to address the extraneous allegations that were not included in the initial motion to strike. =(302/CWW/VC)

Set for hearing on Friday, March 13, 2009, line 11, DEFENDANT PLANT INSULATION COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY AJUDICATION. Motion continued to March 19, 2009. Supplemental briefs due March 17, 2009. =(302/CWW/JW)

rulings.Case Title: BETTE GRANGER VS. ASBESTO DEFENDANTS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, March 13, 2009, line 2, DEFENDANT PLANT INSULATION COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Plaintiffs' motion to compel discovery responses untimely. Plaintiffs' request to continue is denied. Defendant's motion for summary judgment is granted. Plaintiffs have failed to raise a triable issue of material fact. =(302/CWW/JW)

rulings.Case Title: GLORIA KIMBROUGH et al VS. A.W.CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, March 13, 2009, line 3, DEFENDANT PLANT INSULATION COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Motion denied. Failure to shift burden of proof except for negligence cause of action. Triable issue of fact regarding exposure and causation. (Declaration of Plummer, Ay, and Defendant's responses to interrogatories.) Defendant's evidentiary objections overruled. =(302/CWW/JW)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN DOE JR VS. JOY CONDE et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, March 13, 2009, line 4, PLAINTIFF JOHN DOE'S Motion To Disallow Costs. Motion denied. The remittitur is clear. Defendant is entitled to costs. Defendant is awarded $335.68 in costs. =(302/CWW/PB)

rulings.Case Title: SHEILA MULLEN VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Leave Of Court To File A First Amended Complaint Ccp 473, 576; Crc 327
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, March 13, 2009, line 6, PLAINTIFF SHEILA MULLEN'S Motion For Leave Of Court To File A First Amended Complaint, CCP sections 473, 576; and, CRC 327. Plaintiff's motion to file amended complaint is granted. Plaintiff inadvertently failed to plead negligence. Insufficient demonstration of prejudice to defendant. =(302/CWW/VC)

rulings.Case Title: SHEILA MULLEN VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant City And County Of San Francisco'S Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, March 13, 2009, line 6, Defendant City and County of San Francisco's Motion for Summary Judgment. Motion for summary judgment granted. Plaintiff fails to raise a triabgle issue of fact that the MUNI train doors were a dangerous condition of public property. =(302/CWW/VC)
